name: Grade 1 Physical Education — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.title: Practice throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: SHAPE America National PE Standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Physical Education pathway.

units.title: Investigate: throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to throwing, catching, cooperative games, fitness, and personal goals.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
